name: Sanic (Async Python Web Framework)

auth.notes: Framework with built-in JWT/session support via extensions

performance.notes: One of the fastest Python web frameworks; async by default

agent_readiness.notes: Async-first Python web framework focused on speed. @app.route('/') async def handler(request): return json({'hello': 'world'}). Streaming responses. WebSocket support. HTTP/1.1 + HTTP/2. Blueprint for modular routing. Built-in TLS. sanic-ext for OpenAPI, CORS, DI. Worker multiplexing. Built-in CLI: sanic server.app. Faster than Flask/FastAPI for I/O-bound tasks. Less ecosystem than FastAPI but very fast.

gotchas: Less type safety than FastAPI (no Pydantic integration by default)
best_when: High-performance async Python APIs with WebSocket needs
Microservices prioritizing raw throughput over DX
Teams familiar with Sanic from previous projects
avoid_when: New projects (FastAPI provides better DX and ecosystem)
APIs needing automatic OpenAPI docs with validation (FastAPI better)
summary: Sanic is one of the fastest async Python web frameworks with built-in WebSocket and HTTP/2 support. @app.route() with async handlers. Streaming responses. Blueprint routing. Built-in TLS. sanic-ext for OpenAPI/CORS. Worker multiplexing for multi-core. Faster than FastAPI for pure I/O throughput but smaller ecosystem and fewer DX features. Best for high-performance async microservices.
